# Cosmic Signature — Full Documentation

> Cosmic Signature is a strategy bidding game on the Arbitrum blockchain featuring generative NFT art inspired by the three-body problem in physics, ETH prizes, staking rewards, and charitable giving.

## About Cosmic Signature

Cosmic Signature is an NFT project that combines generative art, an exciting bidding game, and social impact through charitable giving. It offers a unique, engaging, and socially impactful experience for the NFT community. The project is deployed on Arbitrum, a Layer 2 scaling solution for Ethereum, to provide fast and affordable transactions while maintaining Ethereum-level security.

The NFT artwork is generated based on the three-body problem in physics — simulating the movement of three planets in space and drawing the trajectories of each planet, creating beautiful and unique patterns. Each NFT is created with a randomly generated seed stored in the smart contract. The image and video are produced from this seed using an open-source Rust program, making every NFT unique and permanently reproducible.

## Website Structure

### Main Pages

- **Home** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/): Live bidding interface showing current round status, prize pool, countdown timer, recent bids, and bid submission form
- **Gallery** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/gallery): Browse and search all minted Cosmic Signature NFTs with filtering and pagination
- **How to Play** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/how-to-play): Step-by-step guide covering game overview, bidding process, reward structure, and pro tips
- **FAQ** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/faq): 50+ questions organized in 6 categories covering getting started, prizes, game mechanics, tokens, technical details, and governance
- **Statistics** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/statistics): Comprehensive game analytics including round history, bid volumes, prize distribution, and player stats
- **Staking** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/staking): Stake Cosmic Signature NFTs (CST) to earn ongoing rewards from the game's prize pool
- **Prize** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/prize): Current and historical prize pool details
- **Contracts** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/contracts): Smart contract addresses, ABIs, and fund distribution breakdown
- **Marketing** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/marketing): Referral and marketing rewards program for promoting the game
- **Mint** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/mint): Interface for minting new tokens
- **Current Round** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/current-round): Detailed view of the active round including bid history, leaderboards, donations, and fund distribution

### User Pages

- **My Tokens** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/my-tokens): View your owned Cosmic Signature NFTs
- **My Staking** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/my-staking): Manage your staked tokens and view staking rewards
- **My Winnings** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/my-winnings): Track your prize winnings across rounds
- **My Statistics** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/my-statistics): Personal gameplay statistics

### Data Pages

- **Winning History** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/winning-history): Complete history of all round winners
- **Named NFTs** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/named-nfts): NFTs that have been given custom names
- **NFT Donations** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/nft-donations): History of NFT donations to the prize pool
- **ETH Donations** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/eth-donation): History of ETH donations
- **Prize Claimed** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/prize-claimed): Record of claimed prizes
- **Charity Deposits** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/charity-deposits-cg): Charitable contribution records
- **Charity Withdrawals** (https://www.cosmicsignature.com/charity-withdrawals): Charitable withdrawal records

## Game Mechanics — Detailed

### Bidding Process

1. Each round begins with a 24-hour countdown and a low initial bid price of approximately 0.01 ETH
2. Every new bid increases the current price by 1% and adds one hour to the countdown timer
3. If you are the last person to place a bid when the timer runs out, you win the main prize
4. Players can bid with ETH or Cosmic Signature Tokens (CST, ERC-20)
5. CST bidding uses a Dutch auction mechanism — the bid price in CST continuously decreases if nobody bids
6. RandomWalkNFT holders can attach their token to a bid for a 50% discount (once per wallet)

### Prize Distribution Per Round

- **25%** — Main prize winner (last bidder when timer expires)
- **19%** — Staking rewards (distributed to all staked token holders)
- **10%** — Charitable beneficiary (chosen by Cosmic DAO)
- **6%** — Raffle winners (4 randomly selected bidders share this pool)
- **Remaining** — Carried over to the next round's pot

### Rewards Per Bid

- 100 CST (Cosmic Signature Tokens) earned per bid
- 1 raffle ticket for end-of-round drawings
- Chance to win a Cosmic Signature NFT

### NFTs Minted Per Round

12 Cosmic Signature NFTs are minted each round:
- 1 for the main prize winner
- 11 for NFT raffle winners (5 bid-based + 4 RandomWalk staker + 2 additional)

### Special Titles

- **Endurance Champion**: The bidder who remained the last bidder for the longest consecutive period
- **Chrono Warrior**: The bidder who held the Endurance Champion title for the longest consecutive period

### Time Mechanics

- Time added per bid starts at 1 hour but gradually increases over time (~10-20% per year)
- This slows round duration growth and limits total NFT supply, maintaining scarcity

## Tokens

### CST (Cosmic Signature Token)

- Type: ERC-20
- Earned: 100 CST per bid placed
- Uses: Alternative bid currency (Dutch auction), Cosmic DAO governance voting
- Tradeable on Uniswap and other Arbitrum DEXes

### Cosmic Signature NFT

- Type: ERC-721
- Art: Generative, based on three-body problem physics simulation
- Storage: Random seed stored on-chain; images reproducible via open-source Rust program
- Supply: Limited by increasing time-per-bid mechanism
- Tradeable on OpenSea and other Arbitrum NFT marketplaces

### RandomWalkNFT

- Partner NFT project
- Holders get 50% bid discount in Cosmic Signature (one-time per wallet)
- Can be staked for additional raffle entries

## Technical Architecture

- **Blockchain**: Arbitrum (Ethereum Layer 2 rollup)
- **Smart Contracts**: Solidity, immutable after ownership renunciation
- **Frontend**: Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
- **Wallet Integration**: RainbowKit, wagmi, supporting MetaMask, Coinbase Wallet, WalletConnect
- **Data**: TanStack React Query for real-time data fetching
- **Monitoring**: Sentry for error tracking, Google Analytics for usage

## Governance & Trust

- Team can adjust game parameters only during round activation windows
- Once stable, ownership will be permanently renounced via renounceOwnership()
- After renunciation, game rules become immutable
- All smart contracts are open-source and verifiable
- The team does not profit directly — all funds go into the immutable smart contract
- If the team disappears, the project is self-sustaining (seeds on-chain, code open-source)

## Community & Support

- **Twitter/X**: https://x.com/CosmicSignatureNFT
- **Discord**: https://discord.gg/bGnPn96Qwt
- **Website**: https://www.cosmicsignature.com

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Getting Started

Q: What is Cosmic Signature?
A: Cosmic Signature is an NFT project that combines generative art, an exciting bidding game, and social impact through charitable giving. It offers a unique, engaging, and socially impactful experience for the NFT community.

Q: How does the bidding game work?
A: Each round begins with a 24-hour countdown and a low initial bid price of around 0.01 ETH. Every new bid increases the price by 1% and adds an hour to the clock. If you are the last person to place a bid when the timer runs out, you win the main prize.

Q: What type of bids are available?
A: It is possible to bid with ETH or Cosmic Signature Tokens (ERC-20). You can also attach a RandomWalk token to the bid and receive a 50% discount on the bid price. The bid price in Cosmic Signature Tokens continuously decreases according to the rules of a Dutch auction.

Q: Can I participate if I don't own any NFTs?
A: Yes, anyone can participate in the Cosmic Signature game by placing a bid. You do not need to own an NFT to bid, but owning a RandomWalkNFT gives you a 50% discount on the bid price.

### Prizes & Rewards

Q: What is the main prize?
A: The main prize includes a unique Cosmic Signature NFT, 25% of the ETH pot, and any donated tokens (ERC-20/ERC-721) from other projects.

Q: What rewards do I get for each bid?
A: Every bid earns you three things: 100 CST (Cosmic Signature Tokens), 1 raffle ticket for end-of-round drawings, and a chance to win a Cosmic Signature NFT.

Q: How does the raffle work?
A: Each bid also serves as a raffle ticket. At the end of each round, the smart contract randomly selects 4 raffle ticket holders who share 6% of the prize pool. Additionally, 5 winners receive a Cosmic Signature NFT, and 4 Random Walk NFT stakers also receive an NFT.

Q: How does staking work?
A: After winning Cosmic Signature Tokens in raffles, you can stake them and continue earning income. Staking pays 19% of the CosmicGame contract balance each round, distributed equally among the owners of all staked tokens.

### Game Mechanics

Q: How does the price increase with each bid?
A: Each bid increases the current price by 1%. This exponential growth adds an exciting strategic element to the bidding game. At the start of a new round, the price resets to approximately 100x lower than the final winning bid.

Q: What is Dutch Auction bidding?
A: When you bid with ETH, you earn 100 CST. These tokens can be used to make another bid through the Dutch auction, where the bid price decreases over time if nobody bids.

Q: What is an Endurance Champion?
A: The bidder who remained the last bidder for the longest consecutive period of time.

Q: What is a Chrono Warrior?
A: The bidder who held the Endurance Champion title for the longest consecutive period of time.

### Tokens & NFTs

Q: What makes Cosmic Signature NFTs unique?
A: Each NFT is created with a randomly generated seed stored in the smart contract. The image and video are produced from this seed using an open-source Rust program based on the three-body problem in physics.

Q: How are the NFT images created?
A: The images are generated based on the three-body problem in physics, simulating the movement of three planets in space and drawing the trajectories of each planet.

Q: Is the NFT supply limited?
A: Yes, due to the gradual increase in time added per bid, the pace of new NFT creation slows over time, effectively limiting the total supply.

### Arbitrum & Technical

Q: What is Arbitrum and why is Cosmic Signature deployed on it?
A: Arbitrum is a Layer 2 scaling solution for Ethereum that speeds up transactions and reduces fees. Cosmic Signature is deployed on Arbitrum to provide a better user experience.

Q: Can I use my existing Ethereum wallet on Arbitrum?
A: Yes, the same private keys are valid on both networks. You just need to adjust your wallet's network settings to connect to Arbitrum.

### Trust & Governance

Q: What controls does the team have?
A: The team can adjust certain game parameters only during round activation windows. Once the project is stable, they will renounce ownership permanently.

Q: How does the team profit?
A: The team does not pocket any money directly. All funds go into an immutable smart contract not controlled by the team.

Q: What if the team disappears?
A: The project is self-sustaining. Since NFT seeds are stored on-chain, anyone can recreate the images using the open-source Rust program.
